What if a #uranium company with a ~US$6M market cap already controlled a multi-million-pound uranium resource in Wyoming and still had substantial exploration upside?

That's why I've been paying attention to Noble Plains Uranium $NOBL / $NOBL.V.

The company's flagship Duck Creek project already hosts a 6.36 Mlb NI 43-101 resource, outlined less than a year after acquisition. Yet only about half of the known 5.25-mile mineralized corridor has been drilled so far.

And that's only part of the story.

The recently announced LOI with UR-Energy could be a significant strategic advantage for the Shirley Basin project:

tweets.text:
There's a concept I love in fitness - “become harder to kill”.

You lift heavy, train combat sports, build your cardio, and more. The goal is to build a resilient body that can excel in any condition. 

Try using this concept for your personal finances.

I've watched too many people take ONE bad hit and their entire financial life spirals.

• Wife leaves. No pre-nup. He loses half.
• A new Claude feature wipes out his company overnight.
• Random kid trips on his lawn. Parents sue.
• Health issue that insurance barely covers, and the treatment eats the savings.
• Car accident where the other driver's insurance doesn't cover the damage.
• Mf'er put too much money into Terra Luna.
• Crazy ex slashes tires
• Hurricane hits but insurance doesn’t cover flood damage.
• Get the picture? Most people’s finances are fragile. 

And when things are finally good, everyone wants to upgrade their lifestyle. But instead of doing that, try maximizing for robustness.

Build your financials in a way that makes you harder to kill. 

• Emergency fund of 1-2 years of expenses. Park it in a money market account.
• Good insurance. Life, health, umbrella. Do your research and don't just grab the bare minimum coverage.
• Asset protection. LLCs, trusts.
• Pre-nup if you're getting married.
• Taxes. Every quarter I get an estimate of my taxes and set it aside. Nothing's more fucking stressful than a tax bill you don't have the cash to cover.
• Know your money like the back of your hand. I track every single penny I spend through YNAB.
• Diversify. Real estate, index funds, gold, bitcoin.
• Separate personal and business assets cleanly. If someone sues the business, they can't touch your home.
• Diversify your jurisdictions. I have friends with multiple passports and bank accounts in different countries. Caribbean, Malta, Turkey, etc.
• Lower your lifestyle. It's hard to kill the guy who's perfectly fine living in a 1-bedroom with a mattress on the floor. It's the guy addicted to validation who can't give up the cars he can't afford until it's too late.

When you're making money, DO NOT TAKE YOUR FOOT OFF THE GAS. Opportunities come and go.

I think to myself…”what am I NOT seeing that could kill me?”

Understand this...shit will happen. If bad things happen it's not "bad luck." That's just how life works.

Winter's always coming. 

Shit WILL happen that's outside of your control.

What you CAN control is being able to take the hits and survive.
Upgrade your defense first. Upgrade your lifestyle later.

tweets.text:
Casey's General Store proves you can sell pizza, gasoline and Zyn pouches, not just high bandwidth memory and cloud infrastructure, to get a big stock move. Here was what they said on the consumer:

“So with respect to the consumer, I would say overall, I think consumers are hanging in there. They’re probably being a little more discerning about where they shop and how they spend their money. But we’re seeing growth across all of the income cohorts. And the way we look at that is below $50,000 a year in income is a low income, $50,000 to $100,000 a year is mid and above $100,000 is higher income. And I’d say that all three, we’re seeing growth, a little bit less so in the lower income. The other two cohorts, which is three quarters of our guest base, are spending comparably to what they’ve been spending on.”

“In terms of specific behaviors, we’re not seeing a lot of change on the inside of the store. We are seeing some change in fuel, but it’s very minor. And it’s all the things that we always talk about. If fuel prices get high, we start to see premium sales dip a little bit. We see sales of ethanol blended fuel go up because it’s a little cheaper. We see gallons per transaction drop a little bit. We see fuel transactions themselves go up because people are coming more frequently. So all of those dynamics are happening right now, but in low single digit percentages. So this is kind of very nuanced behaviors.”

$BEAT is now at $9bn FDV

The only scam coin that went close to $10bn without going to $20bn FDV was RIVER, but it was already extremely volatile and trading with very negative funding rate.

All the other ones went to $20bn => MYX / COAI / AIA / RAVE / LAB

Obviously, the sample is very small and there is no rational reason to expect the pattern to repeat.

But $BEAT has trapped a lot of shorts by being almost up only from $1 to $9, and funding is still not negative, I just have a hard time imagining this collapsing here without going full scam mode.

(As usual, never short a scam coin without a strict invalidation.)

Best since I nailed it back in August of 2025. Potential for one last leg down to around $3,640, but doubt that happens. If it does, expect quick rebound. Hence buying big today, but far out. 

You can today get:

-AEM 240/250 call debits for Mar '27 at .80. 10x opp here
-GDX 125/135 call debits for June '27 at .70. 10x opp.
-NEM 135/140 call debits for Jan '27 at .55
-GLD 470/475 call debits for Nov .26 for .35.

That is over. 

New gold bull market begins TODAY!
